Title: Natural variation of OsGluA2 is involved in grain protein content regulation in rice

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm, especially 15–25 days after flowering; significantly higher at 15 DAF in NIL-qGPC-10H/SL431 allele than NIL-qGPC-10S/Sasanishiki allele
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsGluA2 promoter (~2 kb) in complementation construct; OsGluA2 promoter from Sasanishiki and SL431 used in GUS assays (developmentally active/endosperm-associated promoters)
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm (glutelin precursor synthesized as 57 kDa precursor and cleaved in cytoplasm, as described for glutelins)
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Seed storage protein; glutelin type-A2 precursor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates grain protein content, mainly by increasing glutelin accumulation; also increases albumin, prolamin, total amino acids, and enlarges/increases PB II
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Grain endosperm; protein body II in developing endosperm
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves rice nutritional quality; glutelin has relatively high lysine content and better digestibility for humans
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sasanishiki; SL431 NIL/CSSL background derived from Habataki × Sasanishiki
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsGluA2HET/high-expression allele can be introduced into japonica to improve grain nutritional quality by marker-assisted selection or gene editing; caution that higher GPC can reduce gel consistency and potentially palatability
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm, especially 15–25 DAF
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher GPC is associated with lower gel consistency and possible poorer eating/cooking quality; slight changes in starch and amylose contents
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Evaluated across multiple field environments/years and locations; GPC is environmentally influenced, including nitrogen fertilization and growing environment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GluA1; GluB1; GluC; RAG2; 17-kD prolamin genes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Causal natural variation is in promoter rather than coding region; SNP-1100 in promoter is associated with higher transcription and GPC diversity; alleles classified into OsGluA2LET (low expression) and OsGluA2HET (high expression); OsGluA2 shows pleiotropic effects on grain quality
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Population-scale resequencing/haplotype analysis using 3005 cultivated rice accessions from RFGB and 27 O. rufipogon accessions from OryzaGenome; metabolite/biochemical phenotyping of storage proteins and amino acids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; near-isogenic line comparison; complementation transgenics; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; promoter::GUS assays; transient promoter activity assay in protoplasts; protein fraction analysis; TEM of protein bodies; amino acid profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qGPC-10 was fine-mapped to a 35-kb interval and OsGluA2 (LOC_Os10g26060) was identified as the candidate gene. 2. Complementation increased GPC whereas CRISPR knockout reduced GPC, confirming OsGluA2 as a positive regulator. 3. OsGluA2 mainly elevates glutelin accumulation, but also increases other storage proteins and total amino acids in developing grain. 4. A promoter SNP at -1100 underlies expression divergence between low- and high-expression haplotypes and contributes to indica–japonica GPC differentiation.
